title: calamintha graveolens var. exigua
intro: Calamintha graveolens var. exigua, also known as lesser calamint or mountain calamintha, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Calamintha graveolens var. exigua include relief from digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of herbal preparations.
